19XFB2F73FE078163.description: 2015 Honda Civic SE Sedan, 130,772 miles, all black inside and out. The SE was Honda's Special Edition, slotted right between the base LX and the higher EX, and it is the trim Civic shoppers hunt for. It bundles roughly $1,100 in added equipment over the LX, which is why clean SE models hold value and move fast. Easy on gas, easy to drive, a perfect first car or daily commuter for the North Shore. The AC blows ice cold, so your summer commute stays cool and comfortable. The standout is Honda LaneWatch. Flip your right turn signal and a camera in the passenger mirror shows your entire blind spot on the 7 inch touchscreen, so lane changes feel a lot safer. That same screen runs Display Audio with Bluetooth and smartphone integration, plus a backup camera. The SE also adds automatic climate control, cruise control, push button start, smart entry, USB input, and alloy wheels. Under the hood is Honda's proven 1.8 liter four cylinder paired with a smooth automatic CVT and front wheel drive, EPA rated 30 city and 39 highway. These engines routinely run past 300,000 miles on basic maintenance, so at 130,772 miles this one is barely broken in, and the 2015 Civic earned an IIHS Top Safety Pick. 5J6RM4H3XGL120098.description: 2016 Honda CR-V LX. Remote start, cooled down by the time you enter, AWD, backup cam, and legendary reliability. Silver on charcoal, automatic, 2.4L. A family SUV ready for Chicago summer road trips and year round commuting in the suburbs. The AC blows ice cold, and it is great on gas for an SUV, returning up to 31 mpg on the highway. Key features: 4WD, Honda's bulletproof 2.4L engine, automatic transmission, sport utility 4 door configuration, A/C, power windows and locks, cruise control. Practical cargo space and rear seat room, perfect first family car or commuter. YV4902RS0F2652862.description: 2015 Volvo XC60 T6 R-Design Platinum. 325 HP Turbo, AWD, Paddle Shifters, Harman Kardon Audio, Panoramic Sunroof, Navigation, Adaptive Cruise Control, Full Safety Package. Gray on black, automatic, 3.0L Turbo. A Scandinavian performance crossover that looks refined and drives like a sports car, 0 to 60 in roughly 6 seconds. Surprisingly easy on gas for a 325 horsepower all wheel drive performance SUV, returning up to 24 mpg on the highway. Dual zone climate control blows ice cold, so the cabin stays comfortable through the hottest Chicago summer. Key features: turbocharged 3.0L inline six with 325 HP and 365 lb-ft of torque, all wheel drive, paddle shifters, R-Design sport package with 20 inch alloy wheels, aggressive front grille, rear diffuser and dual exhausts, sport leather seats with heat front and rear, heated steering wheel, panoramic sunroof, Harman Kardon 12 speaker premium audio, navigation, power tailgate, proximity keyless entry, Adaptive Cruise Control with queue assist, Collision Warning with auto brake, Pedestrian Detection, Blind Spot Information System, and Lane Departure Warning. Nearly 9 inches of ground clearance and over 67 cubic feet of cargo with rear seats folded.
